correction.rationale: No correction is needed; the existing mask has appropriate anatomy, side, coverage, topology, and visible-pixel boundaries.

judgments.evidence: The candidate is the anatomically correct right-toes region under the COCO semantic side contract. It covers the complete visible toe area as one connected component and respects the foot_mtp boundary.

observations.contour: The cyan contour follows the toe silhouette and the proximal foot-MTP boundary. The individual toe separations and outer toe tips appear plausibly represented.
observations.full_context: The visible forward foot at the bottom-right corresponds to the character's semantic right side according to the supplied pose-chain evidence; the target toes are clearly visible and unobscured.
observations.mask: The binary mask is a single connected component covering the visible toes, with no evident background or neighboring-foot inclusion. It excludes the main foot/ankle region above the toe boundary.
observations.neighbor_overlap: No meaningful overlap with the adjacent foot, ankle, leg, clothing, or background is visible; the neighboring rear foot remains outside the mask.
observations.overlay: The red overlay aligns with the visible toe pixels and follows the outer dorsal, lateral, and plantar contours without obvious clothing or background contamination.
observations.source_crop: The crop shows the complete visible right-toes region, including the distal portions of all visible toes and their natural outer contours. The proximal cutoff is at a plausible metatarsophalangeal split rather than including the whole foot.
